title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can inject malicious code into production environments, potentially leading to data encryption, service disruptions, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing multi-factor authentication (MFA), access controls, and regular code audits.
Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to monitor for unusual activity in cloud environments and production systems.
Ensure regular backups of critical data are performed and stored offline to facilitate recovery in case of a ransomware attack.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal risk, but its widespread adoption could lead to potential misuse by attackers to identify and exploit vulnerabilities in unpatched systems.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code for potential backdoors or malicious behavior before using it in production environments.
Educate teams on safe usage practices to ensure the tool is not misconfigured or misused.
Monitor for any updates or advisories related to the tool's security and functionality.

title: FacturaScripts Vulnerable to Authenticated Remote Code Execution (RCE) via GIF Image Upload in Product Images
description:
# CVE-2026-42879 - FacturaScripts - Authenticated Unrestricted File Upload via MIME Type Bypass
 
## Summary
 
An authenticated unrestricted file upload vulnerability exists in FacturaScripts' product image upload functionality. An attacker with valid credentials can upload a PHP file disguised as a GIF image (using a GIF89a header), bypassing MIME type validation. The file is stored with its original extension, including executable extensions such as .php.
 
---
 
## Details
 
The vulnerability

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42879.

title: FacturaScripts Vulnerable to Unauthenticated phpinfo() Disclosure via Installer Endpoint
description:
### Summary
An unauthenticated information disclosure vulnerability in the Installer controller allows any remote attacker to trigger phpinfo() on a fresh FacturaScripts deployment by requesting /?phpinfo=TRUE, exposing full PHP configuration, server environment variables (including any database credentials, API keys, or application secrets set as env vars), filesystem paths, and loaded extensions without being authenticated.

### Details
The phpinfo() debug endpoint was intentionally added in c

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42878.

title: FacturaScripts vulnerable to stored XSS via product reference in sales/purchases
description:
## Summary

A stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in the product search modal of sales and purchases documents. An authenticated user with access to the warehouse module can create a product with a malicious reference that executes arbitrary JavaScript in the browser of any other user who opens the product search modal inside an invoice, order, or delivery note.

## Affected files

- `Core/Lib/AjaxForms/SalesModalHTML.php`
- `Core/Lib/AjaxForms/PurchasesModalHTML.php`

## Vuln

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42877.

title: FacturaScripts vulnerable to Reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via Cookie Manipulation
description:
### Summary
A Reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in the fsNick cookie parameter. The application reflects the cookie's value directly into the HTML without sanitization.

### Details
The fsNick cookie is rendered into the DOM without encoding. While the server does reject the modified session and forces a logout, the HTML containing the payload reaches the browser first. This lets the script execute immediately upon load, effectively beating the redirect.

### PoC

1. Log

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute malicious scripts in the victim's browser by exploiting the unsanitized fsNick cookie parameter. This could lead to session hijacking, phishing, or unauthorized actions on behalf of the user. However, the server's forced logout mechanism mitigates the impact to some extent.
recommended_actions: Implement proper input sanitization and output encoding for the fsNick cookie parameter to prevent script injection.
Upgrade to the latest patched version of FacturaScripts if available, or apply vendor-provided security patches.
Conduct a comprehensive security review of cookie handling and DOM rendering processes to identify and mitigate similar vulnerabilities.

title: FacturaScripts Vulnerable to Unstripped Image Metadata (EXIF) Leakage via Library Module File Upload/Download
description:
## Summary

**Fectura Scripts** is an open-source ERP application, a **sensitive information disclosure vulnerability** was identified in the **Library** module's image upload and download pipeline. The application fails to strip EXIF and other embedded metadata from user-uploaded image files before storing them and serving them for download. As a result, any authenticated user who downloads an image from the Library can extract the original uploader's **GPS coordinates, device information, time

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-27892.

title: FacturaScripts Vulnerable to Remote Code Execution (RCE) via Zip Slip in Plugin Upload Mechanism
description:
### Summary
A Critical vulnerability exists in the `Plugins::add()` function. The system fails to properly validate the file paths within uploaded ZIP archives. This allows an attacker to perform a Zip Slip attack, leading to Arbitrary File Write and Remote Code Execution (RCE) by overwriting sensitive .php files outside the designated plugins directory.

### Details
The vulnerability is located in Plugins.php. While the `testZipFile` function attempts to validate that the ZIP contains only one

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary code on the server by exploiting the plugin upload mechanism, potentially leading to full system compromise, data breaches, or service disruption.
recommended_actions: Immediately patch the affected system by applying the latest security update or hotfix provided by FacturaScripts.
Implement strict input validation and sanitize file paths within ZIP archives to prevent Zip Slip attacks.
Restrict file permissions and isolate plugin directories to limit the impact of arbitrary file writes.

title: A vulnerability has been found in router-for-me CLIProxyAPI 6.9.29. Affected by this issue is...
description: A vulnerability has been found in router-for-me CLIProxyAPI 6.9.29. Affected by this issue is some unknown functionality of the file internal/api/handlers/management/api_tools.go of the component API Interface. The manipulation of the argument url leads to server-side request forgery. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-8081.

title: The MQTT broker embedded in Yarbo firmware v2.3.9 is configured to allow anonymous connections...
description: The MQTT broker embedded in Yarbo firmware v2.3.9 is configured to allow anonymous connections with no topic-level read or write ACLs. Any host on the same network can subscribe to sensitive telemetry topics or publish control messages directly to the robot without authentication or authorization of any kind.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7415.

title: Yarbo firmware v2.3.9 contains hardcoded administrative credentials embedded in the firmware...
description: Yarbo firmware v2.3.9 contains hardcoded administrative credentials embedded in the firmware image. These credentials are identical across all devices running this firmware and cannot be changed or removed by end users, enabling trivial unauthorized access to device management interfaces by anyone who knows them.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7414.

title: An Improper Input Validation in Ivanti EPMM before versions 12.6.1.1, 12.7.0.1, and 12.8.0.1...
description: An Improper Input Validation in Ivanti EPMM before versions 12.6.1.1, 12.7.0.1, and 12.8.0.1 allows a remotely authenticated user with administrative access to achieve remote code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows a remotely authenticated user with administrative access to execute arbitrary code, potentially leading to full system compromise, data breaches, and disruption of services.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade Ivanti EPMM to the latest patched versions (12.6.1.1, 12.7.0.1, or 12.8.0.1) to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict administrative access to trusted users and implement strong authentication mechanisms.
Monitor systems for unusual activity and apply intrusion detection/prevention systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: A hidden, persistent backdoor was found in Yarbo firmware v2.3.9 that provides remote,...
description: A hidden, persistent backdoor was found in Yarbo firmware v2.3.9 that provides remote, unauthenticated (or weakly authenticated) access to privileged functionality. The backdoor is undocumented, cannot be disabled via user-facing settings, and survives factory reset and ordinary firmware updates.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7413.

title: An Improper Access Control in Ivanti EPMM before versions 12.6.1.1, 12.7.0.1, and 12.8.0.1 allows...
description: An Improper Access Control in Ivanti EPMM before versions 12.6.1.1, 12.7.0.1, and 12.8.0.1 allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to invoke arbitrary methods.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote unauthenticated attackers to invoke arbitrary methods, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data breaches, or full system compromise.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Ivanti EPMM to the latest patched versions (12.6.1.1, 12.7.0.1, or 12.8.0.1).
Conduct a thorough review of system logs to identify any signs of unauthorized access or exploitation.
Implement additional monitoring and intrusion detection mechanisms to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: An Improper Certificate Validation in Ivanti EPMM before versions 12.6.1.1, 12.7.0.1, and 12.8.0...
description: An Improper Certificate Validation in Ivanti EPMM before versions 12.6.1.1, 12.7.0.1, and 12.8.0.1 allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to impersonate registered Sentry hosts and obtain valid CA-signed client certificates.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5787.

title: An Improper Access Control vulnerability in Ivanti EPMM before versions 12.6.1.1, 12.7.0.1, and...
description: An Improper Access Control vulnerability in Ivanti EPMM before versions 12.6.1.1, 12.7.0.1, and 12.8.0.1 allows a remote authenticated attacker to gain administrative access.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows a remote authenticated attacker to escalate privileges and gain unauthorized administrative access, potentially leading to data breaches, system compromise, or disruption of services.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Ivanti EPMM to the latest patched versions (12.6.1.1, 12.7.0.1, or 12.8.0.1).
Review and monitor access logs for any suspicious activity or unauthorized access attempts.
Implement additional access controls and multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all administrative accounts.

description: A Remote Code Execution vulnerability was found in CODEASTRO Membership Management System v1.0 in /add_members.php. This vulnerability affects the file upload functionality, where improper file sanitization allows attackers to inject malicious files which leads RCE.

title: NPM package query-parser-string 1.0.0 is vulnerable to Prototype Pollution. The package does not...
description: NPM package query-parser-string 1.0.0 is vulnerable to Prototype Pollution. The package does not properly sanitize user supplied query parameters and merges them to the newly created object.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the NPM package query-parser-string 1.0.0 allows for Prototype Pollution, which could enable attackers to manipulate the structure of JavaScript objects. This can lead to denial of service (DoS), data corruption, or potential remote code execution in applications using this package.
recommended_actions: Immediately update or replace the query-parser-string package with a patched or alternative version that addresses the Prototype Pollution vulnerability.
Conduct a dependency audit to identify and mitigate other packages with similar vulnerabilities in your application stack.
Implement input validation and sanitization mechanisms to prevent unsanitized user input from being processed by vulnerable components.

title: A Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability was found in PHPGurukal Hospital Management System v4...
description: A Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability was found in PHPGurukal Hospital Management System v4.0 in the /hospital/hms/edit-profile.php page. This flaw allows an authenticated attacker (patient) to inject a malicious script payload into the User Name parameter, which is stored in the application and later rendered in the doctor s interface.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-36388.

description: Improper certificate validation in Ivanti EPMM before versions 12.6.1.1, 12.7.0.1, and 12.8.0.1 allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to enroll a device belonging to a restricted set of unenrolled devices, leading to information disclosure about EPMM appliance and impacting on the integrity of the newly enrolled device identity.

title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in Webkul Krayin CRM v2.1.5. The application...
description: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in Webkul Krayin CRM v2.1.5. The application fails to sanitize user-supplied input in the comment field during Activity creation on the /admin/activities/create endpoint

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The XSS vulnerability in Webkul Krayin CRM v2.1.5 could allow attackers to inject malicious scripts into the application, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or unauthorized actions within the CRM system.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patch or update to a newer version of Webkul Krayin CRM if available.
Implement server-side input validation and sanitization for all user-supplied data, especially in the comment field.
Conduct a security review and penetration testing to identify and mitigate similar vulnerabilities in the application.

title: CVE-2026-42511 - The BOOTP file field is written to the lease file without escaping embedded double-quotes, allowing
description:
The BOOTP file field is written to the lease file without escaping embedded double-quotes, allowing injection of arbitrary dhclient.conf directives.  When the lease file is subsequently re-parsed by dhclient, e.g., after a system restart, an attacker-controlled field from the lease is passed to dhclient-script(8), which evaluates it.

A rogue DHCP server may be able to execute arbirary code as root on a system running dhclient.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows a rogue DHCP server to inject arbitrary dhclient.conf directives, potentially leading to remote code execution with root privileges. This could compromise system integrity and enable attackers to gain full control of affected devices.
recommended_actions: Apply patches or updates provided by the software vendor to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement network-level protections such as DHCP server whitelisting to prevent rogue DHCP servers from interacting with client devices.
Monitor and audit DHCP traffic for suspicious activity and unauthorized configurations.

description: Open redirect vulnerability exists in Multiple laser printers and MFPs which implement Ricoh Web Image Monitor. When accessing a specially crafted URL, the user may be redirected to an arbitrary website. As a result, the user may become a victim of a phishing attack.

description: A vulnerability was detected in Tenda 4G300 US_4G300V1.0Mt_V1.01.42_CN_TDC01. This impacts the function sub_425A28 of the file /goform/DelFil. title: CVE-2026-7468 - A security vulnerability has been detected in 1024-lab smart-admin up to 3.30.0. This affects an unk
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in 1024-lab smart-admin up to 3.30.0. This affects an unknown function of the file /smart-admin-api/druid/index.html of the component Demo Site. The manipulation leads to improper access controls. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

title: CVE-2026-7447 - A flaw has been found in SourceCodester Pet Grooming Management Software 1.0. This vulnerability aff
description: A flaw has been found in SourceCodester Pet Grooming Management Software 1.0. This vulnerability affects unknown code of the file /admin/update_customer.php. This manipulation of the argument type/length/business parameter validity causes sql injection. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used.

title: CVE-2026-7446 - A vulnerability was detected in VetCoders mcp-server-semgrep 1.0.0. This affects the function analyz
description: A vulnerability was detected in VetCoders mcp-server-semgrep 1.0.0. This affects the function analyze_results/filter_results/export_results/compare_results/scan_directory/create_rule of the file src/index.ts of the component MCP Interface. The manipulation of the argument ID results in os command injection. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. Upgrading to version 1.0.1 is able to mitigate this issue. The patch is identified as 141335da044e53c3f5b315e0386e01238405b771. It is advisable to upgrade the affected component.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7446.

title: CVE-2026-7445 - A security vulnerability has been detected in ZachHandley ZMCPTools up to 0.2.2. Affected by this is
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in ZachHandley ZMCPTools up to 0.2.2. Affected by this issue is some unknown functionality of the file src/managers/ResourceManager.ts of the component MCP Log Resource Handler. The manipulation of the argument dirname leads to path traversal. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7445.
